action.content:
Expand the riddle into a story with a funny tone:

What can you catch but not throw?
A cold

Use * to highlight at least 2 sections in your text. For example: *this is a highlighted text section*.
action.label: context

action.content:
It was a crisp autumn afternoon, and Barnaby, a man whose only athletic pursuit was attempting to catch falling leaves with a net, decided to test his wits against the most elusive creature in the universe. He stood in the middle of his living room, eyes wide, waiting for the perfect moment to demonstrate his unique skill set.

"Alright, Barnaby," he muttered to his empty room, "let's see if you can catch something you can't throw back."

He waited. Nothing happened. Then, a sudden chill swept through the room, causing his breath to fog up instantly. Barnaby gasped, his eyes widening in sheer panic. "Gotcha!" he shouted, wrapping his arms around himself and shivering dramatically. "I've caught *a cold*!"

He spun around, triumphant, ready to show off his new trophy. But then, a mischievous thought struck him. "Wait," he said, his voice trembling slightly. "If I've caught it, surely I can throw it back at the person who gave it to me, right?"

He took a deep, shivering breath, prepared to hurl the invisible illness at the air, but then he remembered the rules of physics and biology. You can't throw a cold; it just... stays. It lingers in your sinuses, refusing to be tossed aside like a used napkin. Barnaby sighed, the wind picking up again, and he had to admit defeat. He was stuck with *a very stubborn cold*, and there was absolutely no way to get rid of it by throwing it somewhere else. He just had to suffer through it, one sneeze at a time.
action.label: response
